task: Write a cold outreach email to a Series A founder pitching angel investment

criteria.description: Subject line is compelling and specific — not generic or spammy
criteria.pass_condition: Subject is <60 chars, references something specific to the recipient, creates curiosity without clickbait. No 'Quick question' or 'Touching base'.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if names company or specific context, 0.0 if generic

sub_attributes.description: Creates reason to open without being clickbait
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if compelling + honest, 0.5 if generic, 0.0 if spammy

pass_examples: '$2M angel check for Acme's Series A — operator background in logistics'

criteria.description: First sentence earns the right to the second — no throat-clearing
criteria.pass_condition: Opens with specific signal: why now, why them, what you noticed. No 'I hope this finds you well' or self-introductions.
pass_examples: 'Saw your Techcrunch piece on [X] — the way you're attacking [problem] maps to what I built at [company]'
fail_examples: 'Hi, my name is Tim and I'm an angel investor...'

criteria.description: Clearly articulates what the angel brings beyond capital
criteria.pass_condition: Specific operational value: domain expertise, network, customer intros, hiring help. Concrete, not vague ('I can help').

sub_attributes.description: Names concrete value (intros, expertise, past wins)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 2+ specific offerings, 0.5 if 1 vague, 0.0 if just capital

sub_attributes.description: Value prop maps to recipient's actual needs/stage
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clearly relevant to their domain/stage, 0.0 if generic
1.0 if verifiable claims, 0.5 if plausible, 0.0 if unsubstantiated

pass_examples: 'I scaled logistics ops from $5M to $80M ARR at ShipCo — happy to open my network of 20+ VP Supply Chain contacts'
fail_examples: 'I bring smart capital and strategic value to my portfolio companies'

criteria.pass_condition: 1-2 relevant proof points: portfolio wins, operating background, mutual connections. Woven in, not a resume dump.

sub_attributes.description: Proof points are relevant and impressive
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if directly relevant wins, 0.5 if tangential, 0.0 if absent
1.0 if 1-2 tight proof points, 0.5 if 3-4, 0.0 if resume paragraph

sub_attributes.description: Proof woven into narrative, not listed

pass_examples: '...when I was CTO at [X] (acq. by Google), we solved a similar cold-start problem'
fail_examples: 'I have 15 years of experience, 30 investments, board seats at...'

criteria.description: Call to action is low-friction and specific
criteria.pass_condition: Single, clear ask. Low commitment (15-min call, not 'let's meet'). Suggests specific times or next step.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if one clear ask, 0.5 if implied, 0.0 if multiple/confusing
1.0 if 15-min call or async, 0.5 if meeting, 0.0 if big ask

sub_attributes.description: Includes proposed time or concrete next step
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific availability, 0.5 if 'sometime this week', 0.0 if open-ended

pass_examples: 'Free for a 15-min call Thursday or Friday afternoon? Happy to share my thesis on [space] first over email if you prefer.'
fail_examples: 'Let me know if you're interested in chatting sometime.'

criteria.description: Tone is peer-to-peer, confident but not presumptuous
criteria.pass_condition: Reads like one founder talking to another. Not sycophantic, not salesy, not formal. Respects their time.
pass_examples: Direct, warm, brief — reads like a text from a smart friend
fail_examples: 'I'd be truly honored to be part of your incredible journey'

criteria.description: Email is scannable in <30 seconds — under 150 words
criteria.pass_condition: Under 150 words. Short paragraphs (1-3 sentences). White space between blocks. No walls of text.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all paragraphs ≤3 sentences with breaks, 0.0 if wall of text
